title: Rei Ayanami
wikitext:
[]: Rei Ayanami             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Jul 17, 2006 8:25 am Rei is really the most intriguing character of all. She was an Eva pilot, a friend of Shinji's, or probably more if she didn't blow herself up. Shinji liked her and probably she wanted him also. A mysterious girl who in her heart adored the Ikari father and son. She seemed to be a very sacrificing person in the series. But on the other hand she was one of the keys to destruction who should be blamed for. But watching the scene which Rei used her life to save Shinji from the attack by the 16th Angel is really very touching and from that moment I seemed to like Rei more than Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Mon Jul 17, 2006 9:08 am Oh, OK....you just started to like Rei more than Asuka, Now? :shock:

[]: Re: Rei Ayanami             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 17, 2006 9:24 am tinmeigut wrote: Rei is really the most intriguing character of all.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Jul 17, 2006 9:36 am You've got a point, Eva Monkey. I think it's straight to the point to say 'Rei is the most superficially intriguing and ultimately the most two dimensional of all the characters in Evangelion'. I seem to understand a little bit after reading your introduction to Rei, but forgive my dull and dumb mind, can you explain more on what you mean by 'superficially intriguing and ultimately the most two dimensional character'? Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Mon Jul 17, 2006 11:40 am Agreed on that introduction, Monkey. Why people like Rei? Easy; She is A)Submissive B)Hawt

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jul 17, 2006 11:40 am I think she's "superficially intriguing" because she is quiet, lives alone, says odd things, and we know almost nothing about her past. It is as if we are told from the beginning, "Hey, look, here is this really mysterious character!" I agree with EvaMonkey that she is "two-dimensional" because she pretty much has nothing going for her other than the fact that she is mysterious. We don't get much information on her thoughts on a wide variety of subjects and characters. I also disagree with EvaMonkey on this point, because she does show sparks of personality. And when she does, they can have major impacts on the show. For example, when Rei II sacrifices herself, leading to the creation of Rei III, who ultimately betrays Gendo and.... well you know the rest.

[]: Re: Rei Ayanami             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jul 17, 2006 12:11 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: tinmeigut wrote: Rei is really the most intriguing character of all.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Mon Jul 17, 2006 3:00 pm She's so well-liked because she's everything mysterious thrown together, in terms of behaviour, appearence and even background. Nothing about her is espically starteling, she just gives you a feeling of 'COOL! '.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jul 17, 2006 3:10 pm Legend of the Past wrote: She's so well-liked because she's everything mysterious thrown together, in terms of behaviour, appearence and even background. Nothing about her is espically starteling, she just gives you a feeling of 'COOL! '.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Tue Jul 18, 2006 12:01 pm DatDude wrote: Umm no most of it is she really sumissive most of the time and fanboy like that in a fwap fantisy

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Tue Jul 18, 2006 2:49 pm DatDude wrote: Legend of the Past wrote: She's so well-liked because she's everything mysterious thrown together, in terms of behaviour, appearence and even background. Nothing about her is espically starteling, she just gives you a feeling of 'COOL! '.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Tue Jul 18, 2006 3:38 pm Rei is quite, some kind of shy, submissive mysterious and sexy with one word OWNAGE ReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiRei ReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiReiRei

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 18, 2006 3:52 pm FauÃŸtin wrote: It is easy to be a golem if you are born to be a golem.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Wed Jul 19, 2006 5:44 am Mr. Tines wrote: FauÃŸtin wrote: It is easy to be a golem if you are born to be a golem.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 19, 2006 11:45 am Legend of the Past wrote: Possibly, but seeing as she was injured on a weekly basis I figured those were painkillers.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Wed Jul 19, 2006 12:31 pm Reichu wrote: Legend of the Past wrote: Possibly, but seeing as she was injured on a weekly basis I figured those were painkillers.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Wed Jul 19, 2006 4:44 pm They were downers.

[]:             Quote Post by Alfonso » Wed Jul 19, 2006 7:52 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: They were downers.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 20, 2006 12:54 am Legend of the Past wrote: Who says those were the same ones?
